title: Is Japan a Model Middle Power?
gist: Canadian PM Mark Carney's WEF speech revived the 'middle power' concept, urging non-great-powers to band together for strategic autonomy against economic-integration-as-weapon tactics, while indirectly rebuking Trump over Greenland and tariffs; the excerpt sets up this framing but is cut off before reaching the article's stated Japan-specific analysis.

insights.claim: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney's speech at the World Economic Forum argued that in recent decades of globalization, great powers have begun using economic integration as a weapon.
insights.speaker: Jio Kamata
insights.so_what: Frames a growing narrative among middle powers that economic interdependence is being weaponized by great powers — relevant context for assessing trade-policy and de-risking trends among non-superpower economies.
insights.quote: great powers have begun using economic integration as weapons
insights.locator: Carney argued that over recent decades of globalization

insights.claim: Carney called on middle powers to band together, including with countries that do not share their values, in order to strengthen their collective strategic autonomy.
insights.speaker: Jio Kamata
insights.so_what: A push for pragmatic, values-agnostic middle-power coalitions would reshape trade and diplomatic alignments outside the US-led bloc — worth tracking for new minilateral groupings.
insights.quote: middle powers should band together – even with countries that do not share their values – to strengthen their strategic autonomy
insights.locator: In order to counter this risk

insights.claim: Carney argued the US-led 'rules-based international order' is, in truth, a fiction that the world must confront.
insights.speaker: Jio Kamata
insights.so_what: A sitting G7 leader publicly disavowing the US-led order's legitimacy is a notable escalation in rhetoric among traditional US allies, signaling widening rifts to watch in alliance cohesion.
insights.locator: he urged the world to confront the fact

insights.claim: Without naming the United States directly, Carney declared Canada would 'stand firmly with Greenland and Denmark' and opposed tariff measures targeting Greenland, an indirect rebuke of Trump's hints of seizing Greenland.
insights.speaker: Jio Kamata
insights.so_what: Concrete evidence of Canada positioning itself against a potential US move on Greenland — a flashpoint worth monitoring for Arctic-security and US-Canada relations risk.
insights.quote: stand firmly with Greenland and Denmark
insights.locator: Without naming the United States outright
